page: Amazon Web Services m8in.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance belonging to the m8in family. It is powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance features 48 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 24 physical cores, utilizing hyper-threading with 2.0 threads per core. It is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture, which offloads virtualization functions to dedicated hardware to maximize compute efficiency.

The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations rely on external Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, making it optimized for network-intensive workloads and high-throughput EBS communication.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8in.12xlarge offers a high-density resource allocation suitable for mid-to-large enterprise workloads without the overhead of local storage maintenance. However, the lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring users to architect solutions around network-attached storage, which may introduce latency compared to local NVMe drives. This instance is designed for general-purpose workloads, including enterprise applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and medium-sized databases.
description: Amazon Web Services m8in.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores and is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but compensates with a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. This network and EBS optimization makes the instance highly capable for data-intensive network transfers. Qualitatively, the m8in.12xlarge provides a balanced resource profile for enterprise environments, though the absence of local NVMe storage requires careful architectural planning for disk-heavy tasks. It is suited for workloads such as web serving, application servers, container orchestration, and database hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8in.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by 3.9 GHz Intel processors.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel processors, high-speed networking, and optimized virtual machine performance on a secure hypervisor.
